danger_signals.meaning: immediate threat requiring evasion or return fire

danger_signals.visual_form: high-tech enemies appearing across futuristic worlds
danger_signals.meaning: escalating difficulty and combat intensity per level progression

danger_signals.visual_form: third area of each world transforms airbike into flying ship with full directional movement
danger_signals.meaning: shift from lateral movement constraints to full 360° danger environment; heightened threat

safety_signals.meaning: low-threat scoring opportunity; respite from combat

safety_signals.visual_form: return to lateral movement constraints after boss defeat
safety_signals.meaning: return to predictable threat envelope; controlled navigation space

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: futuristic training worlds; controlled progression through six worlds divided into four areas each

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: no enemies repeated from level to level; unique boss designs per world
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: high-tech enemy installations across distinct world environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: six distinct worlds with unique visual theming and enemy designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies scale in complexity; no repeated enemy archetypes across levels; boss difficulty escalates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: transition from airbike lateral-movement combat to full-directional flying ship combat in boss areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: movement constraints lifted in third area; player gains full 360° mobility, indicating heightened threat complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: bonus stage with sphere collection replaces combat in fourth area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemy pressure removed; pure resource-gathering activity with numerical point values visible on collectibles

environmental_storytelling.example: each of six worlds has distinct visual identity and exclusive enemy roster; no enemy repetition across levels communicates escalating unknown threats
stunning boss designs per world serve as visual markers of world-state danger; each boss visually distinct, indicating unique threats not faced before
airbike transforms into flying ship in boss area; visual and mechanical shift communicates world-state change from training to critical challenge
consistent high-tech visual language across all worlds establishes coherent Space Force training setting

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: airbike constrained to left-right movement with brake and acceleration controls; fires bullets and missiles
airbike transforms into flying ship with unrestricted omnidirectional movement capability
shifts from combat mode to collection mode; seeks numbered spheres for bonus points
fire projectiles at player; present across four-area world structure with no archetypal repetition
unique per world; require full omnidirectional combat response; presence marks culmination of world-specific challenge

notes: Burning Force communicates world-state primarily through mechanical transformation (airbike↔flying ship), visual uniqueness (no repeated enemies or bosses), and structural progression (six worlds × four areas). The game lacks explicit faction visualization but implies Space Force authority through protagonist training framing and structured combat scenarios. Danger signals rely on enemy presence and projectile fire rather than environmental color/symbol systems. Safety manifests through bonus stage transitions and predictable movement constraints returning post-boss. The game's strength in level design and boss distinctiveness serves dual purpose: visual communication of escalating threat and mechanical gameplay variation.
